items.title: Romanian man jailed for 'distraction fraud' of elderly Australian shoppers

items.title: Woman whose rapist wasn't jailed says the fight for justice was still worth it
items.summary: The Canberra woman says the justice system is unpredictable for victim-survivors but many aspects are worth it.

items.title: Push to rename SA's largest federal seat named after 'contradictory' governor Grey
items.summary: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?

items.title: Truro businesses call for certainty on freight bypass project
items.summary: SA businesses situated along a major thoroughfare say they are holding back on making decisions about their future, amid uncertainty surrounding a slated road project that could divert traffic around the area.

items.title: Child sex allegations against Porepunkah gunman Dezi Freeman aired in coronial inquest
items.summary: Fresh details into the killings of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart and Detective Leading Senior Constable Neal Thompson have been aired in the Coroner's Court.

items.title: This week in history quiz, May 25: Will you land a knockout punch?
items.summary: Which is older — Alien or Star Wars? And which sea creature did the UK and Iceland go to war over?

items.title: Tottenham survives relegation battle, Arsenal lifts EPL trophy as Mohamed Salah and Pep Guardiola bid farewell
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur salvages its Premier League status in the nick of time and Arsenal gets its hands on the trophy as Mohamed Salah and Pep Guardiola say their goodbyes.

items.title: Top spy boss Mike Burgess to give evidence as antisemitism royal commission resumes
items.summary: The head of Australia's spy agency will be the first witness when the royal commission on antisemitism resumes this week.

items.title: Donald Trump's deal-making leaves Iran in the driver's seat and Israel more vulnerable
items.summary: Israel goes from looking like the new regional hegemon to looking like it is under greater threat now than it has in years, while Iran now has room to rebuild its proxies.

items.title: Water scientist sounds alarm over $300m Greater South East Irrigation Scheme near Hobart
items.summary: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.

items.title: Iran war's economic shock is reaching Australian wallets and politics
items.summary: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.

items.title: World-first trial testing whether counterterrorism strategies can prevent domestic homicide
items.summary: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.

items.title: Curtis Mead, a once-promising AFL player, is hitting home runs in Major League Baseball
items.summary: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

items.title: Australians increasingly staying put when it comes to jobs and housing, economic data suggests
items.summary: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.

items.title: Angus Taylor’s migration cap may not cut migration at all
items.summary: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

items.title: From sheep mysteries to housing crisis horror, here's what's in cinemas this month
items.summary: Plus the rom-com of yore returns in Finding Emily, the farmyard mystery that's been delighting audiences, and a gory Hollywood video game adaptation filmed on the Gold Coast.

items.title: How these people's lives have changed since quitting alcohol
items.summary: Here's what you had to say about leaving alcohol behind and how it's impacted your health, friendships and lifestyle.

items.title: James Duckworth the first winner at French Open after injury retirement
items.summary: James Duckworth gets an easy path into the second round of the French Open after his opponent retires midway through the second set.

items.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants
items.summary: A bomb blast has hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the latest major attack claimed by separatist militants.

items.title: Cottesloe beachfront redevelopment to proceed after securing $15 million from federal and local governments
items.summary: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.

items.title: Man charged after allegedly threatening 14yo restaurant worker with knife
items.summary: Police say a 30-year-old man who allegedly demanded money from a 14-year-old worker at knifepoint, on the NSW Central Coast, was tackled to the ground by two other men.

items.title: North Queensland crowd raises record figure for Arrow and MND charity
items.summary: North Queensland climbs into the NRL's top six with an impressive 30-18 victory over South Sydney in Townsville, where fans raise thousands of dollars for the Fight MND charity.

items.title: One dead, 21 feared trapped under rubble after Philippines building collapse
items.summary: Search and rescue operations are underway in the Philippines after a nine-storey building collapsed.

items.title: Man dies after shark attack in Far North Queensland
items.summary: A 39-year-old man has died following a shark attack in Far North Queensland.

items.title: Russian missile strike hits Kyiv, killing four people and injuring 80 others
items.summary: Russia uses a hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile to hit Kyiv, killing four people and injuring at least 80 others.

items.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic
items.summary: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.

items.title: Love story: Teddy needed love, and I knew I could give it to him
items.summary: Judith Geppert has an impressive resume of achievements and celebrations, but her capabilities are often questioned. Her dog Teddy, however, never treated her any differently.

items.title: Sexual Health Victoria to close Melbourne CBD facility despite concerning STI rates
items.summary: The service says government funding has failed to keep pace with increased demand and overhead costs, with rates of sexually transmitted diseases at near-record levels.

items.title: Independent senator David Pocock won't rule out starting party of independents in response to One Nation
items.summary: The independent senator says Australians are frustrated with a political system that "does not work for them".

items.title: WA State Library exhibition sheds fresh light on Albany's pre-colonial history
items.summary: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.

items.title: King Valley prosecco producers prepare for upcoming export label change for Italian wine variety
items.summary: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

items.title: Endangered Manning River turtle numbers holding steady, a year after record-breaking floods
items.summary: Experts are relieved to see the number of endangered Manning River turtles holding steady, a year after devastating flooding in northern New South Wales.

items.title: Endangered river turtles bounce back a year after historic floods.

items.title: V/Line punctuality drops to lowest level in nearly a decade on some lines during free public transport
items.summary: The latest V/Line data reveals only 56 per cent of services arrived on time on two regional train routes during Victoria's first month of free public transport.

items.title: Autumn rain brings boom in birdlife to regional inland South Australia after drought
items.summary: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.

items.title: China coal mine explosion kills at least 82, investigation finds multiple safety violations
items.summary: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.

items.title: Early intervention program teaching kids how to navigate grief
items.summary: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

items.title: Tourism Australia Instagram post belies danger of Western Arthur Range Traverse
items.summary: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

items.title: One Nation facing Western Sydney challenge ahead of state election
items.summary: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

items.title: Farmers take to the stage to leave stress of the land behind
items.summary: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.

items.title: Hull City promoted to EPL after beating Middlesbrough in EFL Championship playoff
items.summary: Riley McGree's Premier League dream is over and the Socceroos star will hope his World Cup hopes are not also dashed after limping off with a hamstring injury in Middlesbrough's Championship playoff final defeat to Hull City at Wembley.

items.title: Holes in Australia's sovereign capability exposed by Iran war supply chain issues
items.summary: The Iran war has not only highlighted the vulnerability of global supply chains — it has also exposed the overlooked parts of Australia's manufacturing sector needed in a crisis.

items.title: Five Italian divers died in a Maldives cave system. Authorities still don't know why they went down there
items.summary: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

items.title: Makri, an abandoned Greek island with bargain price tag and complicated history, to be auctioned
items.summary: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

items.title: Labor has picked a fight on tax, but not all critics are vested interests
items.summary: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.

items.title: Rachel Ward gave up glamour for cows and a not-so-simple country life
items.summary: With the country wind in her grey hair, cow dung on her boots and not a skerrick of make-up, former actor Rachel Ward has found her place, sharing her passion for regenerative farming.

items.title: Visualising the mouse plague infesting parts of Australia
items.summary: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?

items.title: Five economists share their opinion of Labor's budget and the Coalition's reply
items.summary: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

items.title: Netball's Jamie-Lee Price is frustrated. This is what she wants to see change in her sport
items.summary: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

items.title: Quiz: Ready to tickle your brain? Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
items.summary: Know your way around a homonym? Nah, me neither but come in and have a crack at our Sunday quiz anyway.

items.title: These woodworkers at Melbourne Design Week are building on sustainability
items.summary: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

items.title: Auckland FC makes A-League Men history with grand final win over Sydney FC
items.summary: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

items.title: 'Stay with us': Dragons booed off the field at home ahead of fan day
items.summary: St George Illawarra is 0-11 to start the 2026 season after losing to the Warriors in Kogarah, while the Sea Eagles continue their strong form with a hard-fought victory over Gold Coast.

items.summary: The case has been dropped against an Australian artist charged with displaying Nazi symbols on an artwork depicting politicians and billionaires.

items.title: Brent Harvey reflects on losing AFL/VFL games record to Collingwood star Scott Pendlebury
items.summary: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

items.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare
items.summary: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

items.title: Shipbuilding woes and tired submarines — why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'
items.summary: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go announces death of 14-year-old son from cancer in emotional Instagram tribute
items.summary: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

items.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains - ABC News
items.summary: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship
items.summary: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

items.title: Vaccination 'not enough' to contain diphtheria spread in remote WA, disease official says
items.summary: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

items.title: Qld man charged by Joint Counter Terrorism Team for alleged 'concerning posts'
items.summary: A 48-year-old has been arrested by the state's Joint Counter Terrorism Team over alleged "concerning posts" on social media.

items.title: Workforce shortages contribute to psychiatrist's lighter penalty after he sexually harassed nurse
items.summary: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

items.title: 'Life-changing' surf therapy program launched in Queensland to help female veterans
items.summary: A Queensland woman has launched a program designed specifically for women veterans to improve their mental health.

items.title: Bushfires could 'exploit' doormat for entry to your home, fire expert warns
items.summary: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

items.title: Albany's abandoned WWII fuel tanks get new life as global graffiti gallery
items.summary: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.
